name: Waveguide & Microwave Structures
description: Sub-sector template for waveguide, microwave transmission line, and passive microwave component patents. Covers waveguide filters, transitions, substrate-integrated waveguide (SIW), and mmWave passive structures.
contextDescription: Waveguide patents cover passive microwave structures used in base stations, satellite communication, radar, and mmWave 5G systems. Substrate-integrated waveguide (SIW) is an emerging technology enabling waveguide performance in PCB-compatible form factors. Key applications: mmWave antenna feed networks, cavity filters for base stations, waveguide-to-microstrip transitions. Key targets: infrastructure vendors (Ericsson, Nokia, Samsung), mmWave module vendors, satellite companies. While lower volume than handset components, per-unit value is higher for infrastructure.
scoringGuidance: Waveguide structures are used in every cellular base station (cavity filters, antenna feeds).
SIW (substrate-integrated waveguide) patents enable compact mmWave components.
mmWave 5G antenna modules use waveguide-based feed networks and transitions.
Cavity filter patents are relevant to base station RF filtering (but lower volume than handset).
Satellite communication uses waveguide structures extensively — growing market.

questions.question: What waveguide structure does this patent address? Score 8-10 for substrate-integrated waveguide (SIW) or novel planar waveguide architectures, 5-7 for rectangular/circular waveguide improvements, 3-4 for microstrip/stripline with waveguide characteristics, 1-2 for general transmission line.
questions.reasoningPrompt: SIW is the highest-value waveguide variant for modern applications — PCB-compatible.

questions.question: Is this patent applicable to mmWave frequencies (>24 GHz) for 5G NR or satellite? Score 8-10 for mmWave 5G antenna feed or mmWave filter/transition, 5-7 for general high-frequency application, 3-4 for mid-band, 1-2 for low-frequency only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: mmWave 5G and satellite communication are the growth markets for waveguide technology.

questions.question: What passive component does this patent implement? Score 8-10 for waveguide filters or multiplexers (infrastructure value), 5-7 for transitions/couplers/power dividers, 3-4 for simple waveguide sections, 1-2 for no specific component.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Waveguide filters and multiplexers have the highest per-unit value.

questions.question: Is this waveguide structure compatible with standard PCB/LTCC manufacturing? Score 8-10 for standard PCB-compatible (SIW, slot arrays), 5-7 for LTCC or advanced substrate, 3-4 for machined waveguide, 1-2 for exotic fabrication.
questions.reasoningPrompt: PCB-compatible waveguide structures enable mass production — higher infringement volume.

questions.question: How detectable is this waveguide innovation in a commercial product? Score 8-10 for structure visible in X-ray or PCB layout analysis, 5-7 for detectable via module disassembly, 3-4 for internal structure requiring cross-section, 1-2 for design methodology only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Waveguide structures in modules can be detected via X-ray imaging without destructive analysis.

questions.question: What performance advantage does this patent provide? Score 8-10 for significant loss reduction, bandwidth enhancement, or size reduction, 5-7 for moderate improvement in key metrics, 3-4 for incremental improvement, 1-2 for no clear advantage.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Loss, bandwidth, and size are the key metrics for waveguide structures.
